meaning: n.失误,下降,流逝,丧失,过失v.失检,背离,堕入,流逝,失效,下降
examples: He knew that he might soon lapse into unconsciousness.
She had allowed her membership to lapse.
After a lapse of six months we met up again.
Third, there could be a time lapse between selling one portfolio and acquiring another.
This may include a simple mistake, a lapse in judgment or failure to exercise due diligence.
